commands.review_summary:
The branch lowers restart/update probe compatibility to the previous Gateway protocol, redirects package updates to the managed service root, checks the managed service Node before replacement, and updates tests, docs, and changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. From source inspection, current main requires probe clients to satisfy `MIN_PROBE_PROTOCOL_VERSION=4`, so a previous v3 CLI restart-health probe reaches the protocol-mismatch path even though the server has a probe-only compatibility branch.
commands.review_followup: Manual maintainer review is required because the PR has the protected maintainer label, a current-main changelog conflict, and an intentional upgrade-behavior change rather than a narrow automation-only repair.

target.title: Fix managed Gateway updates across CLI and service Node skew
target.body:
Summary:
- Fix the original false-unhealthy update path by allowing old CLI probe-mode restart health checks to connect across a one-version Gateway protocol skew, while keeping normal clients on the current protocol floor.
- Make `openclaw update` treat the managed Gateway service as the source of truth when the shell CLI and service install differ: it now targets the service package root and prints both roots plus the service Node path.
- For future package updates, check the managed Gateway service Node against the target OpenClaw release engine before replacing the package, and run post-update doctor/restart follow-up through that same service Node.
- Document the managed-service update behavior and protocol probe compatibility, with a user-facing changelog note.

User-facing behavior:
- If the service Node can run the target OpenClaw release, `openclaw update` updates the package root used by the managed Gateway service and restarts/verifies that service with a compatible health probe.
- If the service Node is too old, update stops before package replacement and reports the exact Node path that must be upgraded. It does not silently upgrade Node itself.
- If the shell `openclaw` still points at a different install after the update, the command warns so the user can fix PATH or reinstall the service from the intended install.

Verification:
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm test src/cli/update-cli.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm test src/gateway/server.auth.default-token.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/protocol/version.ts src/gateway/server.auth.default-token.suite.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Live local proof with a temporary patched Gateway on port 19879: old 2026.5.7 normal status still fails protocol mismatch, but old 2026.5.7 internal restart-health probe succeeds against the newer Gateway.
target.author: amknight
